sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! SPACE created a G box at rows 16-19, cols 8-11! And the white part shrank from 16 pixels to 4 pixels. The green is still at rows 16-19, cols 13-15.
So SPACE created a G box to the LEFT of the green. This is similar to what happened before - SPACE creates a box. But this time it's G, not c!
Wait, let me think about this. Earlier, when I pressed SPACE:
1. First time: green was at rows 28-31, cols 21-23 (next to center G box). SPACE collected the center G box.
2. Second time: green was at rows 28-31, cols 21-23 (next to left G box). SPACE collected the left G box.
3. Third time: green was at rows 24-27, cols 41-43 (next to top-right c box). SPACE created a new c box at rows 32-35, cols 44-47 (below the top-right box).
4. Fourth time (just now): green was at rows 16-19, cols 13-15. SPACE created a G box at rows 16-19, cols 8-11 (to the LEFT of the green).
So SPACE creates a box when there's no G box adjacent to collect. The color of the created box depends on something. In case 3, it created a c box. In case 4, it created a G box.
Now I have a G box at rows 16-19, cols 8-11. The green is at rows 16-19, cols 13-15. The G box is to the LEFT of the green. If I move LEFT, the green would be adjacent to the G box from the RIGHT side. Then SPACE would collect it!
But wait, I need to understand the pattern better. The G box was created by SPACE. Maybe I should collect it by moving LEFT and pressing SPACE.
Actually, let me reconsider the whole puzzle. The goal seems to be to collect all boxes. I've collected 2 boxes (center and left). Now I have a new G box that I can collect. But there are still 2 c boxes (top-right and bottom-right) that I need to turn G and collect.
Let me collect this G box first, then figure out how to deal with the c boxes.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# SPACE created a G box at rows 16-19, cols 8-11 (to the LEFT of green)
# Green is at rows 16-19, cols 13-15
# White is at rows 16-19, cols 12 (shrunk to 4 pixels)
# 
# The G box is to the LEFT of the green. Move LEFT to get adjacent, then SPACE to collect.

action(['LEFT', 'LEFT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
